rulings.Case Title: GAJANAN INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 1. FINALIZATION OF SETTLEMENT HEARING. On January 12, 2026, Plaintiff filed a notice of settlement. The matter is ordered off calendar. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH)

rulings.Case Title: LIBAH JEAN SHEPPARD VS. SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB INC., A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 2. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF LIBAH SHEPPARD'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The motion to be relieved as counsel is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. VS. OLESYA POVTAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum In Support Of Motion; Declaration In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted. Plaintiff's Motion for Order that Matters in Request for Admission of Truth of Facts be Deemed Admitted is DENIED. The court denies the motion because the defendant states that she provided a response on July 15, 2025, and there is no contention that those responses do not substantially comply with the code. (CCP 2033.280(c) [deny motion where response provided before hearing]; Povtar Decl., par. 3.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE LEIFER VS. SELIGMAN WESTERN ENTERPRISES LTD., A NEVADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 7. PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER, AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One. Plaintiff Adrienne Leifer's motion to compel further responses to Requests for Admission, Set One is DENIED as moot. Defendant Seligman Western Enterprises, Ltd. served amended responses on January 21, 2026. Plaintiff does not challenge the mootness of the motion but still seeks sanctions. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.290(d), "[t] he court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further response, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification in opposing the present motion. Defendant is ordered to pay $1,060 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE LEIFER VS. SELIGMAN WESTERN ENTERPRISES LTD., A NEVADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 7. PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER, AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiff Adrienne Leifer's motion to compel further responses to Requests for Production of Documents, Set One is DENIED as moot. Defendant Seligman Western Enterprises, Ltd. served amended responses on January 21, 2026. Plaintiff does not challenge the mootness of the motion but still seeks sanctions. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 3031.310(h), the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further response to a demand, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification in opposing the present motion. Defendant is ordered to pay $1,060 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE LEIFER VS. SELIGMAN WESTERN ENTERPRISES LTD., A NEVADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 2, 2026, line 7. PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER, AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One. Plaintiff Adrienne Leifer's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One is DENIED as moot. Defendant Seligman Western Enterprises, Ltd. served amended responses on January 21, 2026. Plaintiff does not challenge the mootness of the motion but still seeks sanctions. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(d), "[t]he court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a further response to interrogatories, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification in opposing the present motion. Defendant is ordered to pay $1,060 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable within 10 court days of entry of this order. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
